title: Gold dropped 100 points overnight. I faded the panic. +$126 RR 2.8
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Everyone was bearish on XAU this morning. Rightfully so — the drop was brutal.

But panic moves always leave something behind.

What I saw on the 15M after the flush : — Exhaustion wick into a clean demand zone — Sellers couldn't push further — no follow through — EMA 200 far above — room to breathe on the long — Tight invalidation below the gray box — risk was minimal

Setup before entry : Stop : 0.243% / $11,539 risk Target : 0.683% / $32,363 RR : 2.8

Result : +$126

The move looked obvious in hindsight. It never does in real time.

That's why structure matters more than opinion.

How do you approach trading after a sharp panic drop — fade or wait for confirmation?

comments.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
comments.body:
Running all my setups through an AI tool I've been building before entering.

Upload a chart screenshot, it reads the structure and scores the setup. Stops me from trading the emotion.

This week on XAU alone : +$135 RR 2.15 / +$236 RR 2.0 / +$472 RR 3.24 / +$126 RR 2.8

Free to try : [https://herculmarket.com](https://herculmarket.com)

comments.author: Merchant1010
comments.body: Trump is pulling the strings left and right... unnecessary volatility at current times tbh.

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body: I agree with you on that, but there weren't many opportunities, and I managed to seize it.

comments.author: klymaxx45
comments.body: Interesting concept for a tool. Really neat
